reasoning: A wins on the strength of its two standout concepts no other slot matches: the Parasite Chair (a wearable exoskeleton so you always already wear a seat, inverting the whole logic of distributing furniture through space) and the Mortal Chair (a living organism you feed, water, and eventually bury), both rendered with vivid, load-bearing analogies (air-hockey puck, cast-iron pans, the death of a pet). Its five assumptions are cleanly non-overlapping and the prose is concrete throughout. C ranks second because the Tensile Sling introduces a genuinely new conceptual axis (resisting gravity sideways, routing force up-and-out instead of down through legs) that nobody else touches, and its craft is clean with no process leakage. D is third: beautifully disciplined and concise with the fresh Negotiated Chair (dyadic equilibrium, not crowd-commons) and a tight cradle-to-cradle Last Chair, but several ideas (Atrophy Engine, Inherited Seat) retread A and C. B lands last despite the boldest single idea (the Tithe, a surveillance chair honest about selling your body's data, attacking the commercial rather than physical assumption) because its craft is undermined by leaking task artifacts (a literal "Files read for this task" list with skill paths) that shatter the fiction, and three of its five (Opponent, Negative, Settle) are near-duplicates of competitors' entries.
